title: AUC-safe OOF calibration and regime-specific blending
summary: Scikit-learn notes isotonic can change ROC-AUC because it introduces ties, while sigmoid preserves ranking; beta calibration is a richer parametric map than logistic and often more stable than isotonic.
rationale: Scikit-learn notes isotonic can change ROC-AUC because it introduces ties, while sigmoid preserves ranking; beta calibration is a richer parametric map than logistic and often more stable than isotonic. That makes it a better post-processing candidate for leaderboard-facing blends.
implementation_hint: Generate OOF predictions from 2-4 diverse models, then fit per-regime calibrators on OOF only: start with sigmoid and beta calibration for dry vs wet and early vs late race; reserve isotonic only if it improves OOF AUC despite ties. Blend calibrated OOF scores with a simple linear or constrained-weight stacker.
expected_effect: Can smooth model-specific score distortions and rescue leaderboard generalization without changing base features.
risk: Calibration often helps log loss more than AUC; overfitting the stacker or using in-fold predictions will give false gains.
